Bash(cd /Users/jack/Software/garazyk

# Create all 16 scratchpad files
for i in \
  "01-auth-crypto:Auth & Crypto:Auth/, Auth/Crypto/, Auth/OAuthProvider/, Auth/PDS/, Auth/Verifier/" \
  "02-network-core:Network Core:Network/ (Http*, RateLimiter, SSL*, SSRF*, WebSocketUpgrade)" \
  "03-network-xrpc:Network XRPC:Network/ (Xrpc*, AppViewXRpcRoutePack)" \
  "04-database-storage:Database & Storage:Database/, Core/Repositories/" \
  "05-repository-mst:Repository & MST:Repository/" \
  "06-app-appview:App & AppView:App/, AppView/" \
  "07-admin-ui:Admin & UI:Admin/, AdminUIServer/" \
  "08-identity-plc:Identity & PLC:Identity/, PLC/" \
  "09-sync-relay:Sync & Relay:Sync/ (Firehose, Relay, WebSocket)" \
  "10-services-blob:Services & Blob:Services/, Blob/, Security/" \
  "11-compat-platform:Compat & Platform:Compat/" \
  "12-remaining-sources:Remaining Sources:Chat/, CLI/, Video/, Email/, Ozone/, Metrics/, Debug/, Federation/, Lexicon/, Core/ (non-Repository)" \
  "13-auth-crypto-tests:Auth & Crypto Tests:Tests/Auth/, Tests/Security/" \
  "14-network-xrpc-tests:Network & XRPC Tests:Tests/Network/, Tests/XRPC/" \
  "15-db-repo-core-tests:Database, Repo, Core Tests:Tests/Database/, Tests/Repository/, Tests/Core/, Tests/CharacterizationTests/, Tests/Compat/" \
  "16-app-service-integration-tests:App, Service, Integration Tests:Tests/App/, Tests/AppView/, Tests/Services/, Tests/Integration/, Tests/Interop/, Tests/CLI/, Tests/Blob/, Tests/Email/, Tests/Federation/, Tests/Identity/, Tests/PLC/, Tests/Sync/, Tests/Media/, Tests/Metrics/, Tests/Deployment/, Tests/Lexicon/"
do
  id="${i%%:*}"
  rest="${i#*:}"
  name="${rest%%:*}"
  dirs="${rest#*:}"
  
  cat > ".scratchpad/headerdoc-revision/group-${id}.md" << HEREDOC
# Group ${id}: ${name}

## Directories
${dirs}

## Audit Status
- [ ] Audit complete
- [ ] Rewrite complete

## File Inventory

| File | Quality | Issues |
|------|---------|--------|
| _(populated during audit)_ | | |

## Rewrite Decisions

_(populated during rewrite)_

## Before/After Samples

_(populated during rewrite)_
HEREDOC
done

echo "Created 16 scratchpad files"
ls -la .scratchpad/headerdoc-revision/)

Bash(deciduous add goal "Backfill service topology presets (Indigo Tap, Hydrant, Wintermute)" --confidence 90 2)

Bash(deciduous add decision "Add backfill as a ServiceRole alongside pds/relay/plc/appview. Three backfill implementations: Indigo Tap (Go, SQLite, sync utility), Hydrant (Rust, fjall, native ws://), Wintermute (Rust, PostgreSQL, requires bsky-dataplane sidecar for schema init). Wintermute uses the production pattern from Blacksky: dataplane runs Kysely migrations, Wintermute writes to same DB with ON CONFLICT." --confidence 90 2)

Bash(deciduous add action "Implemented backfill role in topology_registry.ts, three topology presets (indigo-tap.json, hydrant.json, wintermute.json), Dockerfile overlays for wintermute and bsky-dataplane, ws:// scheme support patch for Wintermute, and topologies/README.md documentation" --confidence 90 2)

Bash(deciduous add outcome "All three backfill topologies compile successfully. Dependency chain verified: PostgreSQL → dataplane (migrations) → wintermute (indexing). Memory gotchas updated with backfill service details." --confidence 90 2)
Bash(deciduous link 1644 1650 -t enables -r "Backfill presets use the swappable topology system")

Bash(deciduous prompt 1650 "Backfill service topology presets for cross-implementation interop testing.

Three implementations:
- Indigo Tap (Go): sync utility, SQLite, backfill/verification, port 2480
- Hydrant (Rust): fjall indexer, native ws://, XRPC queries, port 3000
- Wintermute (Rust): PostgreSQL indexer, bsky-dataplane sidecar for schema init, port 9090

Key architectural decision: Wintermute uses bsky-dataplane sidecar (from bluesky-social/atproto) to run Kysely migrations, creating the bsky schema. This is the production pattern from Blacksky (outof-coffee/atproto). Wintermute then writes to the same DB with ON CONFLICT for safety.

Files: docker/wintermute/, docker/bsky-dataplane/, scripts/scenarios/topologies/indigo-tap.json, hydrant.json, wintermute.json, topologies/README.md" 2)
Bash(deciduous add goal "P0: Safety & Correctness" -d "Fix 6 critical issues: withSpanSync, POST retries, unhandledrejection, Deno.exit, stdout pipe, path traversal" -c 95)

Bash(deciduous add observation "Current dashboard has 4+ independent polling loops (2s, 3s, 5s) with no coordination" -d "signals.ts polls /api/runs/active every 2s, RunProgress polls /api/runs/{id}/progress every 3s, NetworkStatus polls /api/network/health every 5s. Toolbar fetches on mount. Each island independently manages its own fetch + setInterval. This creates race conditions and stale state — RunProgress uses window.location.reload() as a sledgehammer to sync on completion." 2)
Bash(deciduous link 49 51 -r "evidence: current architecture is out-of-sync by design" -t leads_to 2)

Bash(deciduous add decision "Single Runtime per island with Msg dispatch" -d "Each Fresh island gets a single Runtime that owns all I/O. The Runtime runs an effect loop: dispatch(Msg) → update(state) → effects. Effects are async functions (fetch, setTimeout, etc.) that produce Msgs. The island's view() reads from state. No island ever calls fetch or setInterval directly — only the Runtime does. This eliminates the scattered polling and makes the state the single source of truth." 2)
Bash(deciduous link 50 52 -r "TEA variant: single runtime per island" -t leads_to)

Bash(deciduous add decision "Dashboard state model: unified DashboardState with per-concern slices" -d "Instead of each island owning its own state, define a single DashboardState type with slices: { network: NetworkSlice, runs: RunsSlice, scenarios: ScenariosSlice, topology: TopologySlice }. The server renders the initial state. The client Runtime hydrates from server props and then only updates via Msg dispatch. Islands read from their slice. This makes it impossible for the sidebar and the main content to disagree about scenario status because they read from the same state." 2)

Bash(deciduous add decision "Effect system: Cmd = tagged union of declarative effects, not raw Promises" -d "Cmds are data, not functions. Example: { type: 'fetch', url: '/api/network/health', onSuccess: Msg.NetworkHealthReceived, onError: Msg.FetchFailed }. The Runtime interprets Cmds into actual I/O. This makes effects testable (you can assert the update function produces the right Cmds without doing I/O) and replayable (you can replay a sequence of Msgs to reproduce any state). The Runtime also handles deduplication — if a fetch is already in-flight for the same URL, it doesn't fire a duplicate." 2)
Bash(deciduous link 52 54 -r "effect system is the Cmd interpreter" -t leads_to)
